title: Scalar Lax-Friedrichs update is monotone under the CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 12.5, p. 232.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If -1 <= cfl <= 1, uLeft <= vLeft, and uRight <= vRight, then the scalar Lax-Friedrichs update at (uLeft, uRight) is less than or equal to the update at (vLeft, vRight).
assumptions: cfl, uLeft, uRight, vLeft, and vRight are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar Lax-Friedrichs update is monotone in both neighboring input values under the CFL condition.

declarations.name: laxFriedrichsStep
declarations.statement: def laxFriedrichsStep (cfl uLeft uRight : ℝ) : ℝ := laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl * uLeft + laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl * uRight

declarations.name: laxFriedrichsStep_monotone
declarations.statement: theorem laxFriedrichsStep_monotone (cfl uLeft uRight vLeft vRight : ℝ) (hcflLower : -1 ≤ cfl) (hcflUpper : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : uLeft ≤ vLeft) (hRight : uRight ≤ vRight) : laxFriedrichsStep cfl uLeft uRight ≤ laxFriedrichsStep cfl vLeft vRight

tasks.input: Translate the scalar Lax-Friedrichs monotonicity statement into Lean 4 using laxFriedrichsStep.
tasks.target: theorem laxFriedrichsStep_monotone (cfl uLeft uRight vLeft vRight : ℝ) (hcflLower : -1 ≤ cfl) (hcflUpper : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : uLeft ≤ vLeft) (hRight : uRight ≤ vRight) : laxFriedrichsStep cfl uLeft uRight ≤ laxFriedrichsStep cfl vLeft vRight

tasks.input: Given the weight nonnegativity theorem and the step definition, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using laxFriedrichsWeights_nonneg, mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left, and linarith.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics theorem needed to prove monotonicity of the Lax-Friedrichs convex combination.
